introduction:
In Delhi, the Caste Certificate for SC, ST, and OBC is issued by the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) office under the Revenue Department, Government of NCT of Delhi. It is mandatory for availing reservations in government admissions, jobs, and Central Government welfare schemes.

Applications can be filed online through the Delhi e-District Portal or offline at the nearest SDM / Tehsil office. OBC certificates in Delhi are particularly governed by the Central OBC list (not Delhi state list).
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Issued by the Sub-Divisional Magistrate (SDM) of the applicant's residential district
  • Processing time: 21 working days (as per Delhi Guarantee of Services Act)
  • Fee: ₹0 (Free) for SC/ST; nominal fee for OBC
  • SC/ST certificates are permanent; OBC NCL certificates renewed annually
  • Online portal: Delhi e-District Portal
  • OBC status is as per Central Government OBC List, not Delhi state list
documentsRequired:
Documents Required

Identity Proof
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • Passport
  • Ration Card
Caste / Nativity Proof
  • Father's / Grandfather's Caste Certificate
  • School Certificate (Class X) showing caste
  • Birth Certificate with caste mentioned
  • Ration Card showing caste (old)
Residence Proof (Delhi)
  • Electricity / Water Bill
  • Rent Agreement (registered)
  • Bank Passbook with Delhi address
Additional (OBC)
  • Income Certificate (Non-Creamy Layer — annual income below ₹8 lakh)
  • Affidavit of OBC Non-Creamy Layer status
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Delhi e-District Portal

Apply at Delhi e-District Portal

  1. Visit Portal
    Go to edistrict.delhigovt.nic.in
  2. Register / Login
    Register using your Aadhaar number and mobile number. Verify via OTP.
  3. Select Service
    Go to Revenue Department → SC/ST/OBC Certificate.
  4. Fill Application Form
    Enter all personal details — name, DOB, caste, father's name, permanent address of native state, and Delhi residence address.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload required documents in prescribed format (PDF/JPG, max 200 KB each).
  6. Submit Application
    Submit the form and save the Application Reference Number.
  7. Track Status
    Track at the same portal or via SMS on your registered mobile number.
  8. Download Certificate
    Once approved by the SDM, download the digitally signed certificate from the portal.
You can also apply via Delhi Janhit Guarantee Act — the certificate must be issued within 21 days, or action can be taken against the responsible officer.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at SDM / Tehsil Office

  1. Visit SDM Office
    Visit the SDM or Tehsil office of your residential district in Delhi.
  2. Collect & Fill Form
    Obtain the Caste Certificate application form from the counter and fill all details clearly.
  3. Attach & Submit Documents
    Attach self-attested photocopies and submit at the counter. Get an acknowledgement receipt.
  4. Verification
    A Revenue Inspector (Patwari) may visit your residence for field verification of caste and address.
  5. Collect Certificate
    Collect your signed caste certificate from the SDM office within 21 working days.
Processing Time: 21 working days (guaranteed under Delhi Janhit Guarantee Act).
afterApplying:
After Applying — What Next?

Track Application

Track at e-District Portal using Application Reference Number.

OBC NCL Renewal

OBC Non-Creamy Layer certificates must be renewed annually. Apply for renewal before the certificate expires.

Grievance / Delay

Call 1076 (Delhi CM Helpline) or file online at PG Portal. If delay exceeds 21 days, file under Delhi Guarantee of Services Act.
